jokes.text: Yep. The moon. It's been here for quite a while.
jokes.setup: Fry asks Leela if she remembers the moon
jokes.punchline: Leela's matter-of-fact response about the moon's longevity
jokes.explanation: Classic Leela deadpan delivery with perfect economy. The understatement of geological time scales is character-appropriate and gets a solid chuckle.

jokes.text: Man, we sure used to try harder back then.
jokes.setup: Romantic nostalgia about their first delivery
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission they don't try as hard anymore
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about relationship entropy. Solid character moment but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: A cherished family keepsake for hours to come.
jokes.setup: Traditional sales pitch about keepsakes lasting generations
jokes.punchline: Honest admission it will only last hours
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of sales pitch expectations. The specificity of 'hours' makes it funnier than a vague short timeframe.

jokes.text: Five years of art school.
jokes.setup: Fry compliments the balloon artist's skill
jokes.punchline: Artist reveals extensive formal education for balloon animals
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between expectation and reality. The specificity of 'five years' adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Ow!
jokes.setup: Balloon animal creation scene
jokes.punchline: Balloon pops and presumably hits or startles Fry
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy. Gets the job done but nothing special or memorable.

jokes.text: Then your mom should have bought you one.
jokes.setup: Bender expresses wanting a corn dog
jokes.punchline: Vendor's harsh rejection treating adult robot like a child
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - sets up like vendor will sell, then delivers unexpected putdown. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: The Mecha-Hexadecapus is about to flail erratically into action.
jokes.setup: Announcing carnival ride
jokes.punchline: 'Flail erratically' as official ride description
jokes.explanation: Excellent word choice comedy. 'Flail erratically' perfectly captures both the mechanical action and the danger in bureaucratic language.

jokes.text: Poor Leela. I never even told her I loved her.
jokes.setup: Fry's dramatic mourning for apparently dead Leela
jokes.punchline: Sets up Bender's immediate contradiction
jokes.explanation: This is mainly setup for the following punchline. Character-appropriate but not funny on its own.

jokes.text: What! You told her, like, 140 times. Yeah, but she paid very little attention to me.
jokes.setup: Fry claims he never told Leela he loved her
jokes.punchline: Bender's specific count reveals the absurd truth
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to series history with specific number '140 times.' Perfect character moment for both Bender's bluntness and Fry's obliviousness.

jokes.text: You gotta live in the moment, baby. And in this moment, Leela's dead.
jokes.setup: Bender offers philosophical comfort
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts it with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - appears to offer wisdom then delivers harsh reality. Excellent structure with the philosophical setup immediately demolished.

jokes.text: The good news is, we found your stuffed animal. What's the bad news? Your wife is dead. She wasn't my wife. Oh, then there is no bad news.
jokes.setup: Classic good news/bad news format
jokes.punchline: Technical correction eliminates the 'bad news'
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of the good/bad news trope. The bureaucratic logic makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Seeing Leela fly off the Hexadecapus and crash through the moon dome and survive inside a stuffed animal by breathing a balloon was a dose of reality.
jokes.setup: Fry describes an incredibly unrealistic sequence of events
jokes.punchline: Calls it 'a dose of reality'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of the most unrealistic scenario possible with 'dose of reality.' The absurd specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Uh-huh. Good talk. We're done here.
jokes.setup: Fry's emotional revelation about proposing
jokes.punchline: Bender's complete disinterest and dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - maximum economy in shutting down emotional moment. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: My little meatbags growing up.
jokes.setup: Bender just dismissed Fry coldly
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually touched by Fry's growth
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Bender's hidden affection through his typical derogatory 'meatbags' term used endearingly.

jokes.text: Joy Juice, hot.
jokes.setup: Professor demonstrates time button
jokes.punchline: Repeats exact same action after going back in time
jokes.explanation: Visual demonstration of time travel concept. Simple but effective for establishing the device's function.

jokes.text: Oh, right. You were outside the button ball, so you don't remember a thing I said.
jokes.setup: Professor expects recognition of his demonstration
jokes.punchline: Realizes the audience missed it due to the time mechanics
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor about the logical consequences of time travel storytelling. Good sci-fi comedy writing.

jokes.text: Friends, I found $10.
jokes.setup: Time loop demonstration
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg repeats exact same announcement
jokes.explanation: Functional callback demonstrating time loop. More plot-serving than comedically innovative.

jokes.text: Say, check out the money I found. It fell off a chump.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg's story about finding money
jokes.punchline: Changes from 'man' to 'chump' showing character growth in confidence
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail showing Zoidberg becoming bolder with each loop iteration. Good attention to character development.

jokes.text: Each time the button sends you back 10 seconds, it takes 10 seconds to recharge.
jokes.setup: Fry asks about using button repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Professor explains the limiting factor
jokes.explanation: More expository than comedic, but the logical constraint creates dramatic irony for later plot developments.

jokes.text: Hell, no. This button is for scientific use only.
jokes.setup: Fry asks about using button for romantic moments
jokes.punchline: Professor's emphatic scientific ethics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Professor's scientific priorities. Sets up dramatic irony since we know Fry will steal it.

jokes.text: Classy choice, chief. She'll crap her pants.
jokes.setup: Fry selects an engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Crude description of classy reaction
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between 'classy' and crude expression. Typical lowbrow jewelry dealer characterization.

jokes.text: That'll be five bills. Hey, give me back my stones.
jokes.setup: Transaction being completed
jokes.punchline: Time reset makes it look like theft
jokes.explanation: Good use of time mechanics for situational comedy. Shows consequences of time manipulation.

jokes.text: Clam, bam, thank you, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Elzar preparing clam dish
jokes.punchline: Rhyming catchphrase for food presentation
jokes.explanation: Typical Elzar showmanship with food-based wordplay. Good character consistency but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: I'll have what she's having.
jokes.setup: Leela chokes on clam during proposal
jokes.punchline: Customer wants same choking experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent timing and misdirection. Classic movie reference applied to choking situation creates perfect dark comedy moment.

jokes.text: Oh, my gosh. This is all so sudden after 13 years.
jokes.setup: Fry proposes marriage
jokes.punchline: Leela calls it sudden after over a decade together
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment highlighting the absurd timeline of their relationship. Great meta-commentary on the show's long run.

jokes.text: The Vampire State Building?
jokes.setup: Fry mentions tallest building in New New York
jokes.punchline: Vampire/Empire State Building pun
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective future-building wordplay. Good world-building humor that's economy-driven.

jokes.text: Strange things happen when the shadows lengthen.
jokes.setup: Fry shows time button to janitor
jokes.punchline: Ominous warning about sunset/time manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good foreshadowing delivered with appropriate ominous tone. Creates dramatic tension while being character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I traveled a thousand years forward in time, but without Leela, I have no future.
jokes.setup: Fry's despair about Leela not showing up
jokes.punchline: Poetic contrast between literal and emotional time
jokes.explanation: Beautiful wordplay connecting the show's premise to emotional stakes. Excellent character moment with poetic language.

jokes.text: Of course. My watch is fast because it kept running every time I hit the button.
jokes.setup: Fry thinks Leela stood him up
jokes.punchline: Realizes time manipulation affected his perception
jokes.explanation: Good logical revelation about time travel mechanics. Shows Fry's intelligence in figuring out the problem.

jokes.text: The universe is caught in a time loop.
jokes.setup: Explaining the cosmic-scale disaster
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery of universe-ending situation
jokes.explanation: Typical Professor casual delivery of catastrophic news.

jokes.text: We'd be shredded across the time-space continuum like human coleslaw.
jokes.setup: Explaining dangers of leaving time shelter
jokes.punchline: Comparing cosmic destruction to coleslaw
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comparison between cosmic horror and mundane food. The specificity of 'coleslaw' makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Yum. Observe.
jokes.setup: Professor describes humans being shredded like coleslaw
jokes.punchline: Bender finds the description appetizing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender reaction - maximum economy in showing his disturbing nature. The casual 'Yum' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Hooray. I'm trapped in a tiny fun room with friends.
jokes.setup: Everyone trapped in time shelter
jokes.punchline: Bender's sarcastic enthusiasm about confinement
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender sarcasm with good character consistency. The 'tiny fun room' description adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Man, falling from a great height an infinite number of times can be stressful.
jokes.setup: Fry stuck in falling loop
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about infinite falling
jokes.explanation: Excellent understatement humor. 'Stressful' is hilariously inadequate for infinite falling death.

jokes.text: At least I have Leela's comforting scream to look forward to.
jokes.setup: Fry stuck in endless falling loop
jokes.punchline: Finds comfort in the recurring terror of his loved one
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny psychological coping mechanism. Shows Fry finding twisted comfort in routine.

jokes.text: It worked. But you killed the Professor. Yeah, but it worked.
jokes.setup: Fry saves himself but kills Professor
jokes.punchline: Fry's priorities showing he values success over Professor's life
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with Fry's skewed priorities. The repetition emphasizes his callousness.

jokes.text: He sure has a lot of blood for a skinny guy.
jokes.setup: Professor lying in pool of blood
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about blood volume relative to body size
jokes.explanation: Good dark observational humor with clinical detachment. The casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Those are great.
jokes.setup: Leela mentions falling dreams where you wake up before hitting ground
jokes.punchline: Fry's enthusiastic agreement about pleasant falling dreams
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's simple appreciation. Contrasts with his current infinite falling situation.

jokes.text: You're just one death away from being saved.
jokes.setup: Amy about to crash into Fry to save him
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to death as step in rescue process
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy treating death as minor inconvenience in rescue plan. Good gallows humor.

jokes.text: Then, you guys form a human bus and crash into me at 40 miles an hour.
jokes.setup: Bender's plan to inflate airbag to save Fry
jokes.punchline: Completely insane method involving human vehicle collision
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist Bender logic. Brilliantly over-complicated solution that's somehow character-appropriate.

jokes.text: First, I'm gonna get back these earrings I loaned Amy three years ago.
jokes.setup: Asked what to do with frozen time
jokes.punchline: Leela's first priority is recovering loaned jewelry
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Leela's practical priorities even in cosmic situation. Very relatable female experience.

jokes.text: I would marry you even if you weren't the last man on Earth.
jokes.setup: Fry proposes in frozen time world
jokes.punchline: Leela subverts 'last man on Earth' trope since he literally is
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of classic romantic line. Perfect timing and context makes it both sweet and funny. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Uh, yes? Absolutely.
jokes.setup: Leela asked if she takes Fry as husband
jokes.punchline: Uncertain then enthusiastic confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's slight uncertainty followed by commitment. The hesitation makes it real.

jokes.text: Leave my wife alone, you glimmer.
jokes.setup: Mysterious glimmer appearing near Leela
jokes.punchline: Fry protecting his wife from unknown light phenomenon
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's protective instincts toward vague threats.

jokes.text: If it keeps bugging us, we'll either kill it or adopt it.
jokes.setup: Mysterious glimmer keeps appearing
jokes.punchline: Leela's binary solution to mystery phenomenon
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic with perfect economy. The binary choice between killing and adopting is hilariously extreme.

jokes.text: And across those oceans.
jokes.setup: Fry mentions walking up 240 flights of stairs
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of walking across oceans too
jokes.explanation: Good throwaway line showing the scope of their frozen time adventures. Casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Boy, did somebody hit you two with an old-and-ugly stick.
jokes.setup: Professor sees aged Fry and Leela
jokes.punchline: Variation on 'ugly stick' saying with age component
jokes.explanation: Good variation on familiar saying. Professor's bluntness is character-appropriate and the addition of 'old' updates the classic insult.

jokes.text: I guess it's good we didn't have children.
jokes.setup: Leela realizes they'll forget everything when time resets
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about hypothetical children's fate
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with disturbing implications delivered matter-of-factly. Shows the weight of their decision.

jokes.text: Lefty loosey, righty tighty, and, fixed!
jokes.setup: Complex time device needs repair
jokes.punchline: Fixed with simple mechanical rule
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between cosmic complexity and simple solution. The familiar phrase grounds the sci-fi moment.

jokes.text: What? I could've fixed it that easily?
jokes.setup: Professor easily fixes complex time device
jokes.punchline: Fry realizes he suffered unnecessarily
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's realization about his unnecessary suffering. Relatable frustration.
